name: Portobello Barley Risotto
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound portobello mushrooms, wiped clean and cut into 1-inch chunks
1/2 cup sweet white wine such as Reisling
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
2 tablespoons flat-leaf parsley, for garnish, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large, heavy pot over medium-high heat, add 2 tablespoons of olive oil. Add the mushroom chunks and cook for 5 to 7 minutes, stirring occasionally. Remove the mushrooms from the pan and set aside.
2. Reduce the heat to medium-low. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of oil and the onion. Cook for about 10 minutes, stirring until the onion is softened.
3. While cooking the onion, bring the vegetable broth to a boil in a medium-sized saucepan. Reduce the heat to a gentle simmer.
4. Add the barley, allspice, and rosemary to the onion. Raise the heat to medium-high and cook for 1 minute, ensuring the barley is evenly coated with oil. Add the wine and cook for 5 minutes, stirring until it nearly evaporates.
5. Gradually add the hot broth to the barley mixture, about half a cup at a time, cooking and stirring frequently over medium heat until each addition of broth is absorbed into the barley before adding the next.
6. When the barley is tender and almost all the broth has been absorbed (about 45 minutes), stir in the reserved mushrooms and raisins. Cook for a few more minutes until the risotto reaches the desired consistency. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve immediately, sprinkling each portion with chopped parsley.
